examples: There would be no sense of peoplehood, none of the untaught affections of those who are part of an organic social unit that shares the same destiny.
How could it, at a time when Europe's scattered and stateless Jews depended on religion to sustain their sense of peoplehood?
There is, however, one group of Israelis that does not share this sense of peoplehood, and which, in a way, cannot share it.
In its central aim of giving the scattered Jews a sense of national peoplehood and providing them with a haven, Zionism has been a brilliant success.
